section.title: Beyond Work
section.subtitle: What I get up to away from the keyboard — and a few things I build for fun.

topics.title: Adventures & Sports
topics.summary: Trails, peaks, volleyball and a good game of football.
topics.body_html: I love the outdoors, and hiking is my thing 🏔️. I've climbed some of the highest peaks in Brazil and once walked a 250 km long-distance trail. My next big dream — which I plan to make happen soon — is to walk the Camino de Santiago. When I'm not on a trail you'll find me playing volleyball 🏐 or, like any good Brazilian, football ⚽.

topics.title: Open Source
topics.summary: Building on each other's solutions so we all get better tech.
topics.body_html:
I love the idea of open source: a solution to one person's problem can become a stepping stone for someone else's — and they can give back the same way. In the end we all benefit from more mature, more capable technology.

Right now I'm contributing to a Django + HTMX component library, where I've been compiling the patterns I reach for every day building real projects.

topics.title: Tools & Pastimes I Build
topics.summary: Taking small everyday annoyances and coding my own fix.
topics.body_html:
I love taking a small everyday problem and building my own solution for it. A couple of them:

  • Quem? — a free, ad-free web version of a "Who am I?" party game I play with friends. The Play Store apps are buried in ads and paywalls, so I built my own.
  • PTAX Converter — in Brazil, invoicing foreign clients means converting amounts with a special Central Bank rate. Their official site is painful, so I took their API and wrapped it in a nicer interface.
